Sthyra · Build-with-Proof

Construction Quality & Dimensional Report

HSR Office, Typical Floor — as-built structural geometry vs the architectural plan

This report compares as-built structural components (slab, walls, doors, rooms) against the architectural plan, combining a photogrammetric 3D model with frame-by-frame visual metrology. Read the capability note first — it defines exactly what these numbers can and cannot certify.

What this method can & cannot measure. The 3D model is a monocular, sparse reconstruction. It reconstructs horizontal surfaces well (floor, desks, the multi-level false ceiling) but the office's blank painted walls produced almost no 3D points, so per-wall plumb, thickness and length are not photogrammetrically measurable here. Wall/door dimensions below are therefore visual estimates from the video using in-frame references (door heights, tiles, switch plates), accurate to roughly ±10–15%. Metric scale is recovered from the plan (±~20%). This is a screening tool that flags gross deviations — it is not a substitute for a laser/total-station/LiDAR measured survey and does not certify mm-level tolerances.
3.32 m
Slab / ceiling height (±~20%)
0.47°
Ceiling out-of-level
0.94°
Floor–ceiling non-parallel
9
Rooms visually assessed

1 · Structural geometry (from the 3D model)

Slab is level & parallel. The dominant floor and ceiling planes are horizontal to within 0.47° / 0.47° and parallel to within 0.94° — i.e. no detectable slab tilt or ceiling slope at screening resolution. Slab-to-slab height measures ~3.32 m (cross-checked to ±~20% against an eye-height scale).

Walls were not reconstructable. Every dominant plane in the cloud is horizontal; the bare walls carried too little texture to form measurable surfaces (see histogram — the 1.4–2.3 m "wall zone" is nearly empty). Per-wall verticality and room dimensions therefore come from the visual pass below, not the geometry.

2 · Door / opening dimensions (visual metrology)

Estimated from the footage using in-frame references; each cross-checked by a second independent estimate. Compare against standard Indian sizes (internal 750–900 × 2030–2100 mm; main 900–1000 × 2100 mm).

3 · Wall & finish quality

LocationWallPlumbFinishFlatnessIssues
Reception / entrance t00 Glazed external wall (window line with glass railing, overlooking road) — N/W sideplumb goodflat Full-height aluminium-mullion glazing reads vertical against the frameless door mullions; glass safety railing aligned. No obvious defects, though severe panoramic distortion limits a strict plumb check.
Reception / entrance t00 White plastered feature wall with faceted/triangulated panels + vertical wooden batten (terracotta) cladding behind reception deskplumb goodflat Geometric panel joints and batten lines look clean and evenly spaced; paint finish even. This is the best-finished surface in the room.
Reception / entrance t00 White wall / column at stair corner near lift (y180)slight-lean poor— Clear snag: raw, unfinished plaster at the vertical jamb/corner edge — loose cementitious patch, uneven hacked edge and a vertical crack/gap along the corner that has not been finished or painted. Skirting/junction at this corner incomplete.
Reception / entrance t00 White return wall / column beside interior opening to workspace (y090)plumb fair— Broad flat painted wall; a faint vertical seam/hairline visible mid-wall and minor patchiness, otherwise acceptable.
reception t02 Glazed facade (long wall, curtain wall over parapet) - wall_y270 / wall_y000 window lineplumb goodflat Aluminium-framed structural glazing with regular vertical mullions reads true-vertical and well-aligned; horizontal transom level. A decorative curved sweep is part of the design (mesh/blind), not a defect. Glass clean, joints regular. No visible defects.
reception t02 Reception feature wall (faceted off-white plaster + vertical wooden batten dado) - wall_y090plumb goodflat Geometric faceted plaster panels with crisp reveal lines; vertical timber-batten cladding to dado is evenly spaced and aligned. Panel joint lines are clean and straight. Apparent skew of panel edges is the intended faceted design, not out-of-plumb construction. Good finish.
reception t02 Entry/back wall with glass door + grey accent (white plaster, grey skirting band) - wall_y180plumb goodflat White painted plaster reads flat under grazing light; door reveal edges vertical. A faint mark/scuff visible on the upper wall but no cracks. Grey base band terminates cleanly. Minor: verify paint touch-up on isolated scuff.
reception t02 Grey accent wall by corridor (smooth render) - wall_y090 right / equirectplumb fairslightly-wavy Grey textured/render finish shows mild tonal mottling typical of textured plaster; slight unevenness under raking light but no cracks or open joints. Acceptable for a feature accent.
reception / curved accent wall t05 Curved accent feature wall (fluted timber dado + render upper, wall y090/y180)plumb goodflat Design centerpiece, well executed: vertical fluted-timber slats have regular consistent pitch; the angular reveal/groove lines between render panels are straight and crisp; timber-to-render diagonal junction is clean. Render (microcement-style) shows mild tonal variation/patchiness which is characteristic of the finish, not a defect. No cracks or open joints seen.
reception / curved accent wall t05 White partition wall with diagonal grey paint feature (wall y180)plumb goodflat Crisp diagonal paint cut-line between white and grey, no bleed; reads plumb against the glass door frame and glazing mullions used as plumb references. Clean paint finish, no visible roller marks or patchiness at this resolution.
reception / curved accent wall t05 Structural glazing / curtain wall to balcony (wall y000/y270)plumb goodflat Full-height slim-mullion glazing with a sweeping curved head reveal (the design curve). Mullions read vertical/plumb; reveals around the glazing are clean. Curved soffit reveal is smooth and even. Fisheye barrel distortion exaggerates curvature at frame edges.
workspace t13 Grey accent / feature wall (boxed column wall holding framed artwork, wall_y180/y090 junction)plumb goodflat Crisp external corner; dark-grey painted finish is even with no visible roller marks or patchiness at this exposure. Vertical arris reads true against the glazing mullions. No cracks seen.
workspace t13 Black-framed glazed partition (cabin enclosure, wall_y180)plumb goodflat Slim steel mullions are vertical and parallel; head transom level. Glass clean. Door pivot/threshold hardware neatly set into floor. Minor reflection only - no obvious gaps or misaligned frames.
workspace t13 White painted plaster perimeter walls + ceiling soffit (wall_y000)plumb fairslightly-wavy Broadly clean white emulsion, but a faint patch/blemish and uneven plaster sheen are visible on the white ceiling soffit near the ceiling fan and around the AC grille; some shadowing suggests minor surface undulation. Cosmetic, not structural.
workspace t18 West glazed wall (y000 / y270 - perimeter glazing)plumb fairflat Curtain track and glazing mullions read vertical (used as plumb lines); apparent lean in equirect is panoramic distortion. Glazing/curtain system intact. Curtains/blinds hang reasonably even. Junction of glazing to floor tile is clean.
workspace t18 Long solid wall behind desks (y270 / y000 white plaster wall with switchboards)plumb poorslightly-wavy Visible blotchy/patchy paint, roller lap marks and sheen/colour variation across the white plaster (clearest in y270 under raking light) - a paint-finish defect. Surface shows mild waviness under grazing light. Switch plates (y000) appear partly finished. No major cracks seen, but finish is below A-grade.
workspace t18 Two-tone feature partition wall with flush door (y180)slight-lean fairflat Two-tone painted partition (white upper / dark grey lower dado). The grey/white waist line is the design intent, but the band line appears slightly off-level where it meets the door reveal. Seen obliquely so lean is low-confidence (panoramic distortion likely). Dark drop-beams/coves are architectural, not defects.
workspace t24 Left long plastered wall (y090) running along window/blind lineplumb goodflat Long white-painted plaster wall; corner and window mullion read vertical (used as plumb lines). Surface flat, clean paint, no visible cracks or patches in the lit zone.
workspace t24 Grey two-tone partitions with flush door + glass partitions (y180/y270)plumb goodflat Two-tone grey/white exposed-look partitions per as-designed intent. Door frame and glazing mullions read plumb. Junction lines between white false-ceiling raft and grey soffit are crisp. Minor snag: loose surface-mounted cable/conduit drooping from ceiling near partitions (y090/y180) and surface ducting; consistent with exposed-look design but the drooping cable loop is untidy.
workspace t24 White ceiling-side walls / soffit junctions (equirect, y270)cannot-tell goodflat Ceiling raft and cove-light strip clean; warm two-tone cove reads as designed. Some surface conduit runs visible (intended exposed look).
cabin t32 Glass-partition wall (cabin / open-office divider, y000 & y180 left)plumb goodflat Slim black steel-framed glazed partition; vertical mullions read true-vertical and parallel, glass panels clean and consistent. Frame joints at head and corner look tidy. No visible gaps or misalignment. The one open door leaf is operational state, not a defect.
cabin t32 Solid painted wall behind sofa / camera-left (y000 left, y090)plumb goodflat White-painted plastered wall, clean and uniform. Corner with ceiling reads sharp and straight. No cracks, patchiness or roller marks evident at this resolution.
cabin t32 Window wall with vertical blinds (y180)plumb goodflat Two windows with vertical-blind treatments; low sill with skirting continuous. Plaster/paint flat. Minor: cabling runs loose along skirting on the floor (housekeeping/wire-management snag, not structural).
cabin t32 Side wall with switchboard & wall-mounted AC (y270, y090)plumb goodslightly-wavy Largely clean white wall. Switch/socket plate set straight. Faint broad shading/undulation visible under raking light near the AC could be very slight plaster waviness or just lighting; not a clear defect. No cracks.
cabin t33 Left solid wall (y000/y270) with wall-split AC and switchboardplumb goodflat Large plain painted partition wall; reads flat and uniformly painted with no visible cracks or patchy plaster. Single modular switch plate mounted ~1.2 m AFL looks level. Minor sheen/lighting gradient only (camera vignetting), not a finish defect.
cabin t33 Glass-partition divider wall (y000/equirect) - black-framed glazed partition + hinged glass doorplumb goodflat Vertical mullions read true/plumb against ceiling and frame. Frame joints (head-to-mullion mitres) look clean. One loose/leaning glass panel propped on the floor inside the far cabin (visible through glazing) is store/snag, not this partition. Door leaf left ajar.
cabin t33 Window wall with corner glazing & vertical blinds (y090/y180)plumb goodflat Wall-to-window reveals and blind tracks appear level/plumb. Trailing power/data cable run loose across the floor below the window and along skirting - workmanship/tidiness snag. Faint dark mark/streak on wall below window sill near socket (possible scuff or adhesive residue). Skirting line continuous and straight.
cabin t34 Left / AC partition wall (solid plaster, white paint) — y000/y270plumb goodflat Clean white painted plaster, no visible cracks. Wall corner (y270) reads vertical against the window-reveal edge. Wall-mounted split AC and modular switch plate mounted neatly. Minor sheen/roller texture only; no defects of note.
cabin t34 Window wall (corner glazing, two adjacent faces with grey vertical blinds) — y090/y180/y270plumb goodslightly-wavy Plaster returns / window reveals look generally straight; reveal jamb in y180 shows very slight plaster waviness and the head-to-jamb junction is acceptable. Skirting band along floor is a marble-look border, clean. A loose cable runs along the skirting at the window (workmanship/cable-management snag rather than structural). Blinds installed level.
cabin t34 Glazed partition wall (black-framed) to open office — y000slight-lean fairflat Mullions read close to vertical but equirect curvature makes plumb hard to confirm — judged from the framed-glass edge. Frame joints look reasonably tight. Cannot fully verify plumb from this distorted view; flagged as slight-lean conservatively, likely acceptable in reality.

4 · Room dimensions vs plan

RoomPlan (as-designed)Visual estimateCheckNote
Reception / entrance t00 23'4" x 17'6" (7.11 x 5.33 m)7×5.2 mconsistent Counting large-format floor tiles (~800 mm) along the long glazed axis gives roughly 8-9 tiles (~6.5-7.2 m), consistent with the 7.11 m plan width. The shorter axis (glass wall to feature/desk wall) reads ~6-7 tiles (~5.0-5.4 m), consistent with the 5.33 m depth. Estimate only — panoramic projection stretches the centre, so treat as a sanity check rather than a measurement.
reception t02 Reception 23'4" x 17'6" (7.11 x 5.33 m)5.4×7 mconsistent The space reads as a long rectangle with the ~7.1m dimension running along the glazed facade and the ~5.3m dimension across to the reception-desk/feature wall. Using 800mm floor tiles and the two-seater sofas (~1.6-1.8m each) as scale, the lounge zone plus circulation plus desk zone are consistent with ~5.3m clear depth and ~7m length. No gross discrepancy vs plan; estimate carries +/-0.5m uncertainty due to 360 distortion.
reception / curved accent wall t05 Reception / accent-wall zone (no explicit numeric plan dim supplied)6.5×5 mconsistent Open reception lounge: estimated ~6-7 m wide (glazing run) by ~5 m deep from accent wall to glazing, using 600mm floor tiles counted across the floor and the ~3.3m slab height as cross-checks. Generous open-plan volume consistent with a reception/lounge. Estimate is rough due to 360 distortion; floor-tile count gives best confidence.
workspace t13 ~41'8" (12.7 m) wide floor plate, open workspace, 4 desk clusters12.5×9 mconsistent Open-plan workspace with long bench desks in 4 clusters spanning the plate; the lateral span of desks + circulation reads consistent with a ~12.7m wide plate. Depth (down the room toward the glazed far-end opening) estimated ~8-10m from desk-row counts; treat as approximate - no boxed dimension was given for depth. Large-format vitrified floor tiles (~800mm) and the slab height (~3.3m by SfM) are consistent with the references.
workspace t18 not provided numerically; AS-DESIGNED = open WORKSPACE, west-side perimeter glazing6.5×12 mconsistent Open-plan workspace consistent with the as-designed intent: long open floor with continuous west glazing and bench-desk clusters. Width (glazing wall to opposite partition) est ~6-7m; depth runs well beyond ~12m into the deeper open-office zone (extent not fully bounded in frame). Slab/false-ceiling height consistent with the ~3.3m slab measured by SfM: false ceiling/cove reads ~2.7-2.9m, exposed dark soffit higher. No plan number to test against, so consistency is qualitative (layout + glazing position match).
workspace t24 not provided (no single plan dimension supplied for this open floorplate)8×14 mconsistent Large open-plan workspace, not a discrete room. Clear width across the desk banks reads ~7-9m; depth down the desk rows is long, >12m (estimated ~14m). Estimated from desk-bank widths (desks ~750mm high, ~1500mm modules) and chair spacing. Reads as a large open floorplate consistent with an open-WORKSPACE as-designed intent. No specific plan dimension was given to verify against, so consistency is qualitative only.
cabin t32 one of 3.23x2.49 m / 3.45x3.53 m / 4.17x2.79 m4.1×2.8 mconsistent The cabin is clearly longer than it is deep: a long ~3m sofa fits comfortably along the glass-partition wall with a wide desk and circulation opposite, and the window wall (y180) shows two windows across the longer run. This reads as the larger elongated option, ~4.17 x 2.79 m (13'8"x9'2"), most consistent. Scale anchored on the ~3m sofa, large-format floor tiles (~800mm modules) and ~3.0-3.3m slab height. The two squarer options (3.23x2.49 / 3.45x3.53) feel too short for the visible furniture run, so 4.17x2.79 is the best match. Estimate +/- ~0.4m given 360-stitch distortion.
cabin t33 as t32 (right-side cabin; exact metres not provided in this brief)3.6×4.2 mconsistent Estimated from furniture scale: a single long desk run (~2.4-3.0 m) with sofa opposite plus circulation reads to a cabin of roughly 3.5-3.8 m x 4.0-4.5 m. Equirectangular projection exaggerates width, so figures are indicative only. Consistent with a standard mid-size director cabin matching the t32 reference; no gross deviation from a t32-sized cabin apparent.
cabin t34 as t32 (right-side cabin; exact plan dimension not supplied in this brief)3.2×4 mconsistent Corner cabin roughly 3.2 m (window-wall direction) x 3.8-4.2 m (depth toward glass partition), estimated from tile module (~800 mm tiles, ~4 tiles across desk run), desk length (~2.4-2.8 m executive desk) and sofa length (~2.0-2.2 m three-seater). Reads consistent with a typical t32-class cabin footprint. No plan number given to check exactly, so consistency is judged on proportion only.

5 · Construction-quality grades

Each area carries an annotated evidence frame with the exact problems boxed and numbered — click any image to expand.

B
Reception / entrance t00
Feature walls, glazing and floor are well executed and the room matches plan dimensions, but a raw unfinished-plaster corner with an unsealed crack and incomplete skirting at the stair/lift junction is a clear as-built snag that drops it from A to B.
▸ click to expand · Stair/lift corner (y180): raw unfinished-plaster jamb with loose cementitious patch and unsealed vertical gap, plus incomplete skirting — set against the well-executed glazing and railing on the right.
A
reception t02
High-spec reception: plumb glazing and feature walls, flat finishes, level floor with clean large-format tiling and well-aligned batten ceiling; only minor cosmetic snags (one wall scuff, slight render mottling) and dimensions consistent with plan.
▸ click to expand · Reception entry/back wall (y180): grade-A finish — clean glass-door reveal and straight batten ceiling, with only minor cosmetic snags (a faint scuff on the white plaster and mild mottling on the grey render accent band).
A
reception / curved accent wall t05
High-quality fit-out: the signature curved/angular accent wall (fluted timber + render) is cleanly executed with straight reveals and regular fluting, glazing and partitions read plumb, tiled floor is level with tight joints, and paint cut-lines are crisp; only minor snags (a dangling cable stub, normal render tonal variation).
▸ click to expand · Curved accent wall (fluted timber + microcement render) is cleanly executed; the only real snag is a dangling looped cable stub and exposed surface conduit left unterminated at the slab near the ceiling, upper-left.
B
workspace t13
Clean fit-out: plumb steel-glazed cabin partition, crisp grey accent corner, level large-format tiling and consistent open-plate geometry; only minor cosmetic plaster blemishes on the white soffit keep it from an A.
▸ click to expand · Open workspace looking along the white plaster soffit: cosmetic plaster blemish and uneven sheen on the ceiling near the fan and AC grille, with desk clutter below; the dark coffered grey soffit and bench layout are cleanly executed.
C
workspace t18
Layout, glazing and ceiling design match the as-designed open workspace, but the long desk-wall shows blotchy/patchy paint with visible roller lap marks (poor finish), a dangling slab cable in the ceiling (y090) and a slightly off-level dado line - cosmetic snags that pull it below B.
▸ click to expand · Open-office ceiling and long plaster wall (y090): a loose cable dangling from the slab soffit, patchy paint on the white wall, and an unfinished switch/conduit box.
B
workspace t24
Walls plumb, surfaces flat with clean paint, false ceiling matches the as-designed two-tone exposed look and reads well-executed; only minor snags (drooping/untidy surface cables, oblique-angle door widths needing tape verification) keep it from an A.
▸ click to expand · Open workspace (t24): clean flat plumb left wall, but a loose cable droops from the ceiling onto the surface-mounted duct and desk cabling is left untidy.
B
cabin t32
Clean, plumb walls, flat finishes, level glossy tiling and a well-fabricated black-framed glass partition; minor snags only (loose floor cabling, slight raking-light wall undulation), so good rather than excellent.
▸ click to expand · t32 cabin, window wall (y180): loose floor cabling along the skirting below the windows is the main snag; faint plaster shading on the AC-side wall under raking light; window wall with level vertical blinds and continuous skirting is cleanly executed.
B
cabin t33
Walls plumb and flat, glazed partition and corner window cleanly finished and matching the t32-cabin intent; downgraded from A only by minor snags (loose cabling, a wall scuff, door left ajar) and limited metrology confidence from the distorted 360 capture.
▸ click to expand · Corner window wall (y180): loose trailing cable along floor/skirting and a faint drip streak below the sill near the socket; blinds and skirting line read clean.
B
cabin t34
Walls plumb and flat, clean paint, square corners, level vitrified flooring and a well-executed glazed partition; downgraded from A only by unmanaged floor/skirting cables and minor plaster/false-ceiling cosmetic irregularities.
▸ click to expand · Corner cabin (t34): exposed/unmanaged cabling on the floor and behind the grey credenza near the window wall is the main snag; the dropped false-ceiling cove line is slightly irregular near the curved corner, while the grey vertical blinds are installed cleanly and level.

6 · Reference tolerances (for context)

Indian benchmarks the as-built would be judged against in a proper survey. This 360 method can only confirm gross compliance/violation — treat green/amber here as "no gross deviation seen", not certified pass.

ElementReference toleranceSourceThis survey can detect?
Wall verticality (plumb)±6 mm / 3 m (max 12 mm)IS 2212 / NBC 2016Only gross lean (>~1.5°)
Wall surface flatness±5 mm / 2 m straightedgeFit-out good practiceOnly visible waviness
Door frame plumb / size±3 mm plumb; ±5 mm sizeIS 4021 / fit-outNo — visual ±10–15%
Room dimensions±10–15 mmFinishes good practiceOnly gross (>~0.2 m)
Floor level±5 mm / 3 mIS 456 / NBCSlab tilt only (~0.5° here)
Slab / floor-to-floor heightper designStructural drawingYes ±~20% (~3.32 m)

Limitations & recommendation

  • Not a measured survey. Wall/door/room figures are visual estimates (±10–15%); metric scale is plan-recovered (±~20%). Use only to flag gross deviations, not for tolerance sign-off.
  • Walls unmeasurable photogrammetrically because they are blank. To get true wall plumb/length/thickness from imagery, either add temporary targets/texture, or capture with a LiDAR scanner (terrestrial scanner, or even an iPhone Pro / iPad Pro LiDAR for room-scale) — that yields mm–cm wall planes directly.
  • Wet areas, pantry and the lift/stair core were never filmed, so their structural geometry is unassessed.
  • For dimensional QC of doors/openings, a 2-minute pass with a laser distance meter beats any image method — recommend that for the elements flagged amber here.
Sthyra as-built pipeline · geometry from COLMAP SfM (307 views, 1.05 px) · door/wall dims from frame-based visual metrology with adversarial cross-check · screening tool, not a certified survey.
